name: Caramel-Orange Buche De Noel
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup firmly packed dark brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
The buche de Noel, or Christmas log, was created in the late nineteenth century by Parisian pastry chefs. This version can be decorated with candied cranberries or fresh currants and enjoyed with a glass of orange liqueur or Sauternes.
8 Servings
1. For Buttercream: Whisk egg yolks, sugar, and flour together in a medium bowl until well combined.
2. Heat half and half to a simmer in a heavy saucepan.
3. Slowly whisk the hot mixture into the egg yolk mixture.
4. Return the mixture to the saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens and boils.
5. Transfer the mixture to a medium bowl. Stir in chocolate and orange peel until smooth.
6. Press plastic wrap directly onto the surface of the buttercream to prevent skin from forming. Let cool completely.
7. Assemble the cake by spreading buttercream on a springform pan lined with parchment paper, arranging pine twigs around the outside, and layering the remaining ingredients in the center.
8. Chill until ready to serve.
